title: NPTEL IoT 2023 JULY

questions.question: Which of the following allows us to identify objects and extract information?
questions.detailed_solution:
RFID Technology allows us to automatically identify and track tags that
are attached to the objects. It extracts information from the tags through electromagnetic
fields.

questions.question: How many layers does Zigbee consist of?
questions.detailed_solution:
Zigbee consists of 4 layers: Physical, Medium Access Control, Network,
and Application.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a component of cloud computing?
questions.detailed_solution:
Cloud computing components include clients, services, applications,
platform, storage, and infrastructure.

questions.question: Which of the following is a distance measuring sensor module?
questions.detailed_solution:
HC-SR04 is the distance measuring module ultrasonic sensor, which
measures the distance between 2cm~450cm.

questions.question: Which of the following is a component in a typical sensor network?
questions.detailed_solution:
A typical sensor network comprises of sensor nodes, routers, gateway,
and sink.

questions.question: Which of the following sensors are responsible for measuring orientation and angular velocity?
questions.detailed_solution:
A gyroscope is responsible for measuring orientation and angular
velocity.

questions.question: "ISA 100.11A" is a wireless networking technology standard. ISA stands for _______.
questions.detailed_solution:
ISA100.11a is a wireless networking technology standard developed by
the International Society of Automation (ISA).

questions.question: Which of the following is not a difference between traditional data center and cloud computing?
questions.detailed_solution:
Major differences between traditional data center and cloud
computing include scalability, flexibility, elasticity, automation, running costs, and
security

questions.question: Smart grid is also known as the energy internet.
questions.detailed_solution: Smart grid is also known as the energy internet.

questions.question: Can a point of node failure result in the partition of the network in the stationary sensor network?
questions.detailed_solution:
If there is a failure in the stationary sensor network then it is likely that
the point of failure can partition the network into two or more fragments.

questions.question: Which of the following are the enablers of IoT?
questions.detailed_solution:
The enablers of IoT are -
a. RFID
b. Nanotechnology
c. Sensors
questions.reference: See lecture 1 (Introduction to IoT - Part - I) @ 12:41

questions.question: Which of the following is/are NOT a characteristic of IoT?

options.text: Abundance of sleeping nodes, mobile and non-IP devices

questions.detailed_solution:
The characteristics of IoT are -
a. Efficient, scalable and associated architecture
b. Unambiguous naming and addressing
c. Abundance of sleeping nodes, mobile and non-IP devices
questions.reference: See lecture 1 (Introduction to IoT - Part - I) @ 16:02

questions.question: State whether the following statement is True or False. Statement: The increasing number of devices in IoT is expected to result in an address crunch.
questions.detailed_solution:
The increasing number of devices in IoT is expected to result in an
address crunch.
questions.reference: See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - II) @ 01:19

questions.question: State whether the following statement is True or False. Statement: The gateway has a unique network prefix, which can be used to identify them globally.
questions.detailed_solution:
The gateway has a unique network prefix, which can be used to identify
them globally.
questions.reference: See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - II) @ 6:58

questions.question: Sometimes, when there is a need for the nodes to communicate directly to the Internet, ______________ can be used.
questions.detailed_solution:
Sometimes,there is a need for the nodes to communicate directly to the
Internet. This is achieved by tunneling.
questions.reference: See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - II) @ 11:44

questions.question: In _______________ a node/network is connected to multiple networks for improved reliability.
questions.detailed_solution:
In multi-homing, a node/network is connected to multiple networks for
improved reliability.
questions.reference: See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - II) @ 15:22

questions.question: The IPv6 notation uses ___________________ values.
questions.detailed_solution: The IPv6 notation uses hexadecimal values.
questions.reference: See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - II) @ 16:33

questions.question: A _____________ detects (senses) changes in the ambient conditions or in the state of another device or a system, and forwards or processes this information in a certain manner.
questions.detailed_solution:
A sensor detects (senses) changes in the ambient conditions or in the
state of another device or a system, and forwards or processes this information in a certain
manner..

questions.question: A microphone is an example of an _____________.
questions.detailed_solution: A microphone is an example of an input device and transducer.

questions.question: The ________________ of a sensor is the smallest change it can detect in the quantity that it is measuring.
questions.detailed_solution:
The resolution of a sensor is the smallest change it can detect in the
quantity that it is measuring.
.

questions.question: Based on the data type, sensors are classified as _______________________.
questions.detailed_solution:
Based on the data type, sensors are classified as Scalar and
Vector/Multimedia.

questions.question: Solenoid valve is an example of ______________________.
questions.detailed_solution: Solenoid valve is an example of actuator.

questions.question: An actuator requires a ______________________ and ______________________.

options.text: Control signal and a bias signal
Control signal and a source of energy
Noise signal and a source of energy

text: Control signal and a source of energy

questions.detailed_solution: An actuator requires a control signal and source of energy.
questions.answer_display: Control signal and a source of energy

questions.question: State whether the following statement is True or False. Statement: Pneumatic rack and pinion actuators are used for valve controls of water pipes.
questions.detailed_solution:
Pneumatic rack and pinion actuators are used for valve controls of water
pipes.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a function of an IoT gateway?
questions.detailed_solution:
Switching, routing, and protocol conversion are the functions of an IoT
gateway.
questions.reference: See lecture 5 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part - I) @ 19:27

questions.question: The full form of MQTT is
questions.detailed_solution: The full form of MQTT is Message Queue Telemetry Transport.
questions.reference: See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 01:51

questions.question: In MQTT, a ________________ controls the publish-subscribe messaging pattern.
questions.detailed_solution:
In MQTT, a message broker controls the publish-subscribe messaging
pattern..
questions.reference: See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 03:33

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a component of MQTT?
questions.detailed_solution:
The three components of MQTT are -
a) Publishers
b) Subscribers
c) Brokers
questions.reference: See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 04:50

questions.question: ______________ is an extension of MQTT which uses lightweight attribute based encryption. It has ___________ main stages.
questions.detailed_solution:
SMQTT is an extension of MQTT which uses lightweight attribute
based encryption. It has four main stages.
questions.reference: See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 13:45

questions.question: Which of the following is based on Request-Response model between end-points.
questions.detailed_solution: CoAP is based on Request-Response model between end-points.
questions.reference: See lecture 7 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part III) @ 00:31

questions.question: The two sub-layers of CoAP are -
questions.detailed_solution:
CoAP has two sub-layers which are -
a) Messaging
b) Request/response
questions.reference: See lecture 7 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part III) @ 04:44

questions.question: Which of the following is used for real-time exchange of structured data?
questions.detailed_solution: XMPP is used for real-time exchange of structured data.
questions.reference: See lecture 7 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part III) @ 11:28

questions.question: XMPP uses the ________________ architecture.
questions.detailed_solution: XMPP uses the client-server architecture.
questions.reference: See lecture 7 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part III) @ 12:03

questions.question: With respect to AMQP, which of the following message delivery guarantees allow for each message to be delivered certainly as well as to be delivered multiple times.
questions.detailed_solution:
The message delivery guarantees of AMQP are -
 At-most-once - each message is delivered once or never.
 At-least-once - each message is certain to be delivered, but may do so multiple
times.
 Exactly-once - message will always certainly arrive and do so only once.
questions.reference: See lecture 8 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part IV) @ 05:35

questions.question: Which of the following is/are NOT an AMQP frame type?
questions.detailed_solution: Open, close, and end are valid frame types of the AMQP protocol.
questions.reference: See lecture 8 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part IV) @ 06:34

questions.question: Which of the following is/are NOT the function/functions of the Bindings component of the AMQP protocol?

options.text: Receives messages and routes them to queues
Separate queues for separate business process

questions.detailed_solution:
The Bindings component of the AMQP protocol manages the rules for
distributing messages (who can access what messages, destination of the message)
questions.reference: See lecture 8 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part IV) @ 08:05

questions.question: Which of the following is/are NOT exchange types in AMQP?
questions.detailed_solution:
The AMQP exchange types are -
a) Direct
b) Fan-out
c) Topic
d) Header
questions.reference: See lecture 8 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part IV) @ 08:56

questions.question: State whether the following statement is True or False. Statement: The IEEE 802.15.4 is a well-known standard for low data-rate Wireless Personal Area Network (WPAN).
questions.detailed_solution:
The IEEE 802.15.4 is a well-known standard for low data-rate Wireless
Personal Area Network (WPAN).

questions.question: The networking topologies supported in the IEEE 802.15.4 are -
questions.detailed_solution: The networking topologies defined in IEEE 802.15.4 are star and mesh.

questions.question: State whether the following statement is True or False. Statement: Periodic transmission of beacon messages does not occur in beacon enabled networks (IEEE 802.15.4).
questions.detailed_solution:
Periodic transmission of beacon messages occur in beacon enabled
networks (IEEE 802.15.4).

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false. Statement: Wired HART lacks a network layer.
questions.detailed_solution: Wired HART lacks a network layer.

questions.question: The HART physical layer is derived from ____________ protocol and operates only in __________ GHz ISM band.
questions.detailed_solution:
The HART physical layer is derived from IEEE 802.15.4 protocol and
operates only in 2.4 GHz ISM band.

questions.question: State whether the following statement is True or False Statement: Super-frames in HART consist of grouped 20ms wide timeslots.
questions.detailed_solution: Super-frames in HART consist of grouped 10ms wide timeslots.

questions.question: _________________ identifies channels consistently affected by interference and removes themfrom use.
questions.detailed_solution:
Channel Blacklisting identifies channels consistently affected by
interference and removes them from use.

questions.question: The _________________ supervises each node in the network and guides them on when and where to send packets.
questions.detailed_solution:
The Network manager supervises each node in the network and guides
them on when and where to send packets..

questions.question: NFC is designed for use by devices within _________ to each other?
questions.detailed_solution:
NFC is designed for use by devices within close proximity to each
other.

questions.question: ______________ contain information which is readable by other devices, however it cannot read information itself.
questions.detailed_solution:
Passive NFC devices contain information which is readable by other
devices, however it cannot read information itself.

questions.question: NFC devices work on the principle of ___________________?
questions.detailed_solution: NFC devices work on the principle of magnetic induction.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a mode of operation NFC?
questions.detailed_solution:
There are primarily three modes of operation in NFC as -
a. Peer-to-peer
b. Read/Write
c. Card emulation

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false Statement: Paging in Bluetooth is the process of forming a connection between two Bluetooth devices.
questions.detailed_solution:
Paging in Bluetooth is the process of forming a connection between two
Bluetooth devices.

questions.question: There are ______ modes of operation in Bluetooth.
questions.detailed_solution: There are 4 modes of operation in Bluetooth.

questions.question: Zwave uses __________ for signaling and control?
questions.detailed_solution: Zwave uses RF for signaling and control.

questions.question: Which of the following is/are not a constraint on sensor nodes?

options.text: Not be adaptive to the environment

questions.detailed_solution:
Sensor nodes -
a. Must consume extremely low power
b. Be adaptive to the environment

questions.question: ___________ are simply those that are unable to perform an operation; this could be
questions.code_block: because of power failure and environmental events.
questions.detailed_solution:
Failed nodes are simply those that are unable to perform an operation;
this could be because of power failure and environmental events.

questions.question: Dumb behavior of sensor nodes is _______________ in nature (as it is dependent on the effects of the environmental conditions).
questions.detailed_solution:
Dumb behavior of sensor nodes is Temporal in nature (as it is dependent
on the effects of the environmental conditions).

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false. Statement: Coverage in WSN is defined as the area-of-interest is covered satisfactorily.
questions.detailed_solution:
Coverage in WSN is defined as the area-of-interest is covered
satisfactorily.

questions.question: If transmission range >= 2* sensing range,
questions.detailed_solution:
If transmission range >= 2* sensing range, coverage implies
connectivity.

questions.question: The two types of reporting in WSN are -
questions.detailed_solution: The two types of reporting in WSN are Event driven and on demand

questions.question: The objective of coverage in WSN is to use a _______________ number of sensors and _______________ the network lifetime.
questions.detailed_solution:
The objective of coverage in WSN is to use minimum number of
sensors and maximize the network lifetime.

questions.question: State true or false. The disadvantages of stationary Wireless Sensor Networks are - Statement-I: Node failure may result in partition of networks. Statement-II: Topology cannot change automatically.
questions.detailed_solution:
The disadvantages of stationary Wireless Sensor Networks are -
a. Node failure may result in partition of networks.
b. Topology cannot change automatically.

questions.question: A _________________ in MWSN moves in order to collect data from sensor nodes and goes to the sink and delivers the collected data from different sensor nodes.
questions.detailed_solution:
A mule in MWSN moves in order to collect data from sensor nodes
and goes to the sink and delivers the collected data from different sensor nodes.

questions.question: ________________ allows distributed sensing carried by humans and the goal is not just to collect data but to allow the common people to assess and share the knowledge.
questions.detailed_solution:
Participatory sensing allows distributed sensing carried by humans and
the goal is not just to collect data but to allow the common people to assess and share the
knowledge.

questions.question: Which of the following is/are NOT feature/features of UAV networks?
questions.detailed_solution:
The following are the features of UAV networks -
a. Mesh or star networks
b. Multi-tasking
c. Large coverage area

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a UAV network constraint?
questions.detailed_solution:
The following are UAV network constraints -
a. Frequent link breakages
b. Prone to malfunction
c. Very Complex

questions.question: In _____________________ configuration, UAVs form multiple star topology. One node from each group connects to the ground station.
questions.detailed_solution:
In multi-star configuration, UAVs form multiple star topology. One
node from each group connects to the ground station.

questions.question: The full form of FANET is?
questions.detailed_solution: The full form of FANET is Flying Ad Hoc Network.

questions.question: Low-end sensor nodes are -

options.text: Whose deployment has high density in order to increase network lifetime and survivability
Who perform basic functions such as data aggregation, auto configuration, and power saving

questions.detailed_solution:
Low-end sensor nodes are -
a. Whose deployment has high density in order to increase network lifetime and
survivability.
b. Who perform basic functions such as data aggregation, auto configuration, and
power saving.

questions.question: Which of the following are the components of the M2M ecosystem?
questions.detailed_solution:
The components of the M2M ecosystem are as follows -
a. Device Providers
b. Internet Service Providers (ISPs)
c. Platform Providers
d. Service Providers
e. Service Users

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false Statement: The M2M Device Platform enables access to objects connected to the Internet anywhere any time.
questions.detailed_solution:
The M2M Device Platform enables access to objects connected to the
Internet anywhere any time.

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false Statement: The M2M Application Platform provides integrated services based on device collected data-sets.
questions.detailed_solution:
The M2M Application Platform provides integrated services based on
device collected data-sets.

questions.question: Company ABC manufactures a room temperature monitor which sends data via protocol X and company MNO manufactures another kind of room temperature sensor which sends data via protocol Y. With respect to this, which among the following correctly captures the said scenario?
questions.detailed_solution:
IoT is characterized by heterogeneity where different kinds of devices, each
communicating with different set of protocols are designed. Refer first part of

questions.question: When two IoT devices wish to communicate, semantic conflict in such a scenario in IoT interoperability refers to____________________.

options.text: Two devices built by the same manufacturer
Two devices sensing the same physical parameter
Two devices having different deployment location
Two devices having different processing and business logic

text: Two devices having different processing and business logic

questions.detailed_solution:
Semantic conflict refers to when different IoT devices have different processing
and business execution logic.
questions.answer_display: Two devices having different processing and business logic

questions.question: Which of the following issues needs to be addressed while solving user interoperability?
questions.detailed_solution:
All of the options given should be addressed while solving user
interoperability issues.

questions.question: Which UMB interoperability component is responsible for converting physical devices into virtually abstracted ones?
questions.detailed_solution: UMB-A is responsible for converting physical devices into virtually abstracted ones.

questions.question: A Protocol Translation Unit (PTU) acts as a middleware between two IoT devices with different native protocols to enable them communicate with each other by translating the language of one device to the other one and vice versa.
questions.reference: Refer Lecture 21@22:16 and onwards where PTU has been explained.

questions.question: Suppose that a smart CCTV camera has been configured using C++ language. With respect to the device's cosign identification as per the standard definition (A,B,C,D), which among the following the information "Configuration Lang:C++" will be most appropriately mapped ?
questions.detailed_solution:
For a device's cosign (A,B,C,D), 'D' refers to the definition of the object, hence is the
most appropriate to which configuration language, which may be considered a definition will be mapped.

questions.detailed_solution:
An Arduino UNO board contains several components, which also contain the

questions.question: What is the series of micro controller chips powering Arduino UNO boards?
questions.detailed_solution:
Arduino UNO micro controllers are powered by ATMEGA series. Refer to any
standard documentation on Arduino UNO.

questions.question: In Arduino IDE the 'Verify' and 'Upload' buttons perform the exact same task.
questions.detailed_solution:
In Arduino IDE, 'Verify' compiles the sketch and checks for its correctness,
while 'Upload' uploads the sketch to the Arduino board.

questions.question: Suppose that an Arduino UNO board is connected to a pneumatic sensor which sends tyre pressure as floating point numbers. Which among the functions will you use to read from the sensor?
questions.detailed_solution:
As per the basics of Arduino programming. When you have real
numbers in analog format, you use analogRead().

questions.question: Which among the following can also be described as a relay, which is an actuator?
questions.detailed_solution: Relay is a type of electro-mechanical switch and is also an actuator.

In python programming, python provides network services for client-server

questions.detailed_solution: Raspberry Pi GPIO acts as both digital output and digital input. (Please refer to

questions.question: Which of the following exits the nano editor in the terminal?
questions.detailed_solution: Ctrl+X exists the editor.

questions.question: Does Raspberry Pi provide configuration options for cameras ?
questions.detailed_solution: Raspberry Camera can be configured using raspi-config.

questions.question: Does python provide a module for pi-camera ?
questions.detailed_solution:
Raspberry Camera can be configured using the Python camera module
python-picamera.

questions.question: Which of the following converts energy to motion?
questions.detailed_solution: Actuator converts energy to motion.

questions.detailed_solution:
Raspberry Pi devices act just like mini computers and can be configured to perform
many things. Refer to the lectures on Raspberry Pi

questions.detailed_solution:
GPIO stands for General Purpose Input/Output. Refer to any standard
documentation on Raspberry Pi. Also visit

questions.question: The Python program which you execute on Raspberry Pi to read data from sensors and control actuators has the same syntax and style as any other Python program.

questions.detailed_solution:

known.
questions.answer_display: Both server IP address and port number

questions.question: Which among the following functions do you use while using MATPLOTLIB to add title to a plot?
questions.detailed_solution: The title(<name_of_title>) functions adds title to a plot while using matplotlib.

questions.question: In traditional non software-defined network consisting of a network of switches, suppose OSPF is being used as the routing protocol. In this context which among the following is true.

options.text: All the switches execute OSPF distributively

text: All the switches execute OSPF distributively

questions.detailed_solution:
Traditional networks are distributive, hence all the switches of the network
execute OSPF.
questions.answer_display: All the switches execute OSPF distributively

questions.question: In SDN, the Operating System (OS) is separated (i.e not strongly coupled) from the physical hardware for each switch.
questions.detailed_solution:
SDN separates the physical and logical plane of the switch. So OS (part of
logical plane) is separated from the hardware (part of the physical plane) for each switch.

questions.question: Which among the following is a popular protocol implementing SDN?
questions.detailed_solution:
OpenFlow by ONF is the most popular protocol implementing the principles of
SDN. Refer to any standard documentation on OpwnFlow.

questions.question: With respect to Software Defined Networking (SDN), which among the following is true?

options.text: SDN couples the data plane and control plane
SDN has no relation to either data plane or control plane
SDN separates the data plane and control plane

text: SDN separates the data plane and control plane

questions.detailed_solution:
SDN separates the data plane and control plane. It is the fundamental principle
of SDN.
questions.reference: Refer Lecture 33 from 12:17 onwards, SDN Architecture.
questions.answer_display: SDN separates the data plane and control plane

questions.question: Consider the following figure below. To which issue of SDN does this particular figure can be related to?
questions.detailed_solution:
The given figures shows the tabular structure of how flow rules are installed within
SDN switches, so it pertains to flow rule placement issues.

questions.question: Suppose that there are two LANs, each configured to be SDN enabled with their own set of switches and controller. Which among the following directional APIs will be used for communication between the two controllers?
questions.detailed_solution:
East-Westbound APIs are responsible for communication between different SDN
controllers and domains.

questions.question: Which among the following is true?

options.text: Backup Controllers have no use is SDN
There is no difference between the main controller and backup controller
Backup controllers take over when the main controller goes down
It is not a good idea to keep backup controllers

text: Backup controllers take over when the main controller goes down

questions.detailed_solution:
Backup Controllers are required to take over the network control when the main
controller fails.
questions.answer_display: Backup controllers take over when the main controller goes down

questions.question: IoT being data intensive and having a lot of security concerns, it is a good idea to integrate SDN with IoT to mitigate many of these issues.
questions.detailed_solution: SDN can be integrated with IoT to reap several benefits of both.

questions.question: Traditional Wireless Mobile Networks are cost expensive in terms of _____________?
questions.detailed_solution:
Traditional wireless mobile networks are cost expensive both with respect to capital
expenditure (CAPEX) and Operational Expenditure (OPEX).

questions.question: High mobility of users is one of the key challenges in rule placement for software defined wireless mobile networks.
questions.detailed_solution:
Dynamic user mobility restricts the ease with which rules can be placed for software
defined wireless mobile networks.

questions.question: Which among the following is a solution for mobility-aware flow rule placement in SDIoT?
questions.detailed_solution:
Mobi-Flow has been proposed to provide a solution for mobility-aware flow rule
placement.

questions.question: Cloud, Utility, Grid and ______________________ have been the major trends in computing
questions.detailed_solution: Cloud, Utility, Grid and Cluster computing are some of the major trends in computing.

questions.question: Cloud computing models allow different users to share the same physical resources?
questions.detailed_solution:
Cloud computing uses virtualization heavily and thus it allows different users to share
the same underlying physical infrastructure.

questions.question: As per NIST Visual Model of Cloud Computing, 'Hybrid Cloud' model and PaaS model both fall in the same category.
questions.detailed_solution:
'Hybrid Cloud' is an example of cloud deployment model while 'PaaS' is an
example of cloud service model.

questions.question: When you are accessing Google docs or Microsoft Word online for accessing the features of a document writing software from your browser without specifically installing them, which among the following cloud service models is the most appropriate one that you are using.
questions.detailed_solution:
This is an example of SaaS, since you are accessing a word/document processing
software as a client over the network. The actual software itself runs on some remote cloud server (Please
refer

questions.question: An organization A wants to deploy a cloud infrastructure, whereby it wants to push majority of the data to a cloud whose servers can be situated anywhere within the globe, but it wants certain private data to be pushed only to cloud servers that are present on-premise and are accessible by only authenticated members of the organization. In this context which among the following deployment model should be used?
questions.detailed_solution:
Hybrid cloud deployment model supports both the features of public and
private cloud.

questions.question: Cloud services and resources should be rapidly ______________ in order to achieve their required performance
questions.detailed_solution:
Rapid elasticity is one of the key requirements for cloud computing paradigms and
models.

questions.question: Suppose that a start-up wants to enter into online book delivery business. It wants to create the user interface and the application by itself, but it does not have any networking or storage facility, so it wants to adopt the cloud computing practice. In this respect, which among the following cloud service models is most suitable for the start-up.

options.text: Both PaaS and IaaS can be adopted

text: Both PaaS and IaaS can be adopted

questions.detailed_solution:
Since both IaaS and PaaS provides infrastructure and storage support to clients, either of
PaaS or IaaS can be employed.
questions.reference: Refer Lecture 38@3:00, comparision of different models.
questions.answer_display: Both PaaS and IaaS can be adopted

questions.question: Data security and client authentication is an issue in which of the following cloud service models?
questions.detailed_solution:
Security is a pertinent issue in all of the cloud service models, which includes SaaS,
PaaS and IaaS. Refer to any standard discussion on challenges and issues on cloud computing and

questions.question: Network, ___________________ and application level securities are the three aspects of cloud infrastructure securities.
questions.detailed_solution:
Network, host and application level securities are the fundamental aspects of cloud
infrastructure securities.

questions.question: When a new user logs into a cloud framework, the first thing that needs to be done is ______________________________.

options.text: User should immediately be granted all the resources

questions.detailed_solution:
User authentication is one of the primary security issue and any user which logs into
the cloud framework must first be authenticated.

questions.question: Service Level Agreements (SLAs) are mutually agreed by the client (customer) and cloud service provider (CSP) at the beginning. With respect to SLAs, what are the views that SLAs provide?

options.text: Both Customer and CSP Point of View
Neither Customer nor CSP Point of View

text: Both Customer and CSP Point of View

questions.detailed_solution:
SLAs provide both the customer point of view and the CSP point of view between the
customer and the CSP. Refer Chapter 10 (Page 245) of "Introduction to IoT" by Sudip Misra, Anandarup
Mukherjee, Arijit Roy, Cambridge University Press.
questions.answer_display: Both Customer and CSP Point of View

questions.question: CloudAnalyst simulation tool provides GUI and is written on top of CloudSim modules.
questions.detailed_solution: CloudAnalyst is built on top of CloudSim and also provides a GUI.

questions.question: Which component of OpenStack do you use to access all the other components?
questions.detailed_solution:
Horizon is the dashboard of OpenStack which provides the GUI and from where you
can access other components.

questions.question: Which among the following is NOT a component of OpenStack?
questions.detailed_solution:
Plasma is not a component of OpenStack. The rest are various components, including
Nova, Glance, Swift etc.

questions.question: Sensor cloud is simply dumping and organizing of sensor data on cloud computing platforms.
questions.detailed_solution:
Sensor cloud incorporates many aspects and not simply dumping of senor data over
cloud platforms.

questions.question: Which among the following are limitations of traditional Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs)?
questions.detailed_solution:
For traditional WSNs, procurement, deployment and maintenance are all critical issues
that needs to be taken care of.

questions.question: In a typical sensor cloud architecture, the sensor cloud infrastructure that provides the virtualization lies ______________________________

options.text: At the same layer as physical sensor devices
In between the physical sensor layer and the application layer
Sensor cloud does not support virtualization

text: In between the physical sensor layer and the application layer

questions.detailed_solution:
In a typical sensor cloud architecture, the sensor cloud infrastructure that provides the
virtualization lies in between the physical sensor layer and the application layer.
questions.answer_display: In between the physical sensor layer and the application layer

questions.question: Generally speaking, Sensor Cloud Service Providers (SCSPs) are also always the owners of the physical sensors
questions.detailed_solution:
SCSPs are providers of the virtualized sensor services, but they may not the owners of
the physical sensors in a typical deployment.

questions.question: In a typical sensor cloud architecture with virtualization, one virtual sensor can be associated with how many physical sensors?
questions.detailed_solution:
One instance of a virtual sensor in sensor cloud architecture can be mapped
with one, two or more physical sensors below.
questions.reference: Refer Lecture 43, Optimal composition of virtual sensors

questions.question: Sensor virtualization aims to achieve more _________________________ in providing sensor based services
questions.detailed_solution:
Sensor virtualization and sensor cloud architecture are designed to provide
more flexibility and convenience than traditional sensor based services. Refer to the lectures on
Sensor Cloud and its motivation closely.

questions.question: Suppose that you want to start a business to provide some IoT based application, but you do not have the means to develop the application layer logic, neither you have the means to purchase and deploy physical sensors. You can however, rent cloud servers for use and write interface logic for interfacing with other modules. Which among the following actors will be the most suitable for you?
questions.detailed_solution:
SCSPs provide the sensor virtualization layer in between the application layer and the
physical sensor layer and thus this is the best business model as per the requirements and constraints given.
Refer to the standard architecture of sensor cloud and utility of SCSPs.

questions.question: Which among the following is implemented along with sensor cloud to make its services and performance better?
questions.detailed_solution:
Caching is very important in sensor cloud and is implemented along with sensor cloud
to make the performances better.

questions.question: Which among the following cases is most likely to reduce the overall price of sensor-cloud implementation provided that the data traverses through multiple sensor hops starting from the origin sensor to the sink node, and provided that all owners are honest and charge for only what is required?

options.text: If physical sensors are owned by multiple owners with high profit margin
If all physical sensors are owned by a single owner with uniform profit margin

text: If all physical sensors are owned by a single owner with uniform profit margin

questions.detailed_solution:
If all the physical sensors are owned by a single owner, then the single owner will
charge an uniform price for data traversing through the sensors. On the other hand, if multiple sensor
owners are there, different owners will charge different rates, which may increase the price. Refer pricing
in sensor-cloud,
questions.answer_display: If all physical sensors are owned by a single owner with uniform profit margin

questions.question: Fog computing is aimed to replace cloud computing completely and has no scope for integration with cloud
questions.detailed_solution:
Fog computing is designed to assist and compliment cloud based technologies in
providing better services, not remove cloud all together.
questions.reference: Refer Lecture 44@3:46 onwards, Introduction to Fog, especially how it is connected to cloud computing.

questions.question: Suppose data from an IoT device first goes to Fog layer for some basic processing, after which it goes to Cloud layer for advanced processing, then the processed data comes back to the Fog layer and then, it finally comes back to the origin sensor node. In the return journey no processing of data takes place anywhere, just transfer of data takes place. If 'Tf' is the time taken by the data to travel from sensor to fog and vice versa, and 'Tc' is the time taken by the data to travel from fog to cloud and vice versa, 'Tfp' is the data processing time at fog and 'Tcp' is the data processing time at cloud, what is the total round trip time 'T' taken by data starting from the origin sensor node, processing the data and then back to the sensor node after being processed.
questions.detailed_solution:
Since data comes back to the origin sensor node, the traversal latency Tf and Tc will be
counted twice. But, since processing takes place only once during onward journey, processing delay is only
one time. Therefore, T = 2(Tf + Tc) + Tfp + Tcp. Refer to the lecture on cloud latency,

questions.question: Consider the standard Fog computing architecture. In which of the following layer will 'very time sensitive data' be processed?
questions.detailed_solution:
Since the data is very time sensitive, it is required that the nearest fog node processes
the data so that immediate action can taken. This is the benefit of having fog computing. Refer to Working
of Fog,

questions.question: "Network, Accelerator, Compute and Storage" constitute the part of which view of fog computing architecture, as defined by OpenFog Consortium Architecture Working Group?
questions.detailed_solution:
Fog Computing Architectural framework has several views, node, system and software
among them. Network, Accelerator, Compute and Storage" fall under Node View. Refer Chapter 11 (Page
260) of "Introduction to IoT" by Sudip Misra, Anandarup Mukherjee, Arijit Roy, Cambridge University
Press, 2021.

questions.question: Data fusion enables optimum utilization of massive data gathered from multiple sources.
questions.detailed_solution:
Data fusion enables optimum utilization of massive data gathered from
multiple sources.

questions.question: Which of the following is a stage in data fusion?
questions.detailed_solution:
Decision level, feature level, pixel level and signal level are the four stages of
data fusion.

questions.question: Which of the following is one of the challenges of Data fusion?
questions.detailed_solution:
The challenges of data fusion involves Imperfection, Conflicts and
Ambiguity.

questions.question: Which of the following is one of the theory of evidence-based mathematical methods of data fusion?
questions.detailed_solution:
Belief function is one of the theory of evidence-based mathematical methods of
data fusion.

questions.question: DLNA stands for Digital Living Network Alliance.
questions.detailed_solution: DLNA stands for Digital Living Network Alliance.

questions.question: Which of the following are the functional layers in smart parking?
questions.detailed_solution:
Information collection, System Deployment and Service
Dissemination are the three functional layers in smart parking

questions.question: Which of the following statements are true about the HAN standards? Statement I: Physical and MAC layers are defined by IEEE802.15.4. Statement II: Network layer is defined by Zigbee. Statement III: Application layer is defined by IEEE802.15.4
questions.detailed_solution:
Physical and MAC layers are defined by IEEE802.15.4. Network layer
and Application layers are defined by Zigbee.

questions.question: Which of the following are true about X-10? Statement I: It allows remote control of compliant transmitters. Statement II: It has low speed and data rate. Statement III: It is adopted by GE.
questions.detailed_solution:
X-10 allows remote control of compliant transmitters, has low speed and
data rate and is adopted by GE.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. ____________enables vehicles to wirelessly share a diverse range of information.
questions.detailed_solution: V2X enables vehicles to wirelessly share a diverse range of information

questions.question: Which of the following is a property of Vehicular Ad-hoc Networks?
questions.detailed_solution:
VANETs are based on DSRC and WAVE. They also have
guaranteed low-latency in mobile environments.

questions.question: Knob is a switch type of INN.
questions.detailed_solution: Knob is a switch type of INN.

questions.question: Which of the following is responsible for central coordination in Body-brain architecture?
questions.detailed_solution:
The brain is responsible for central coordination in Body-brain
architecture

questions.question: Which of the following stands for DSRC?
questions.detailed_solution: DSRC stands for Dedicated Short Range Communication

questions.question: Ad-hoc is a domain of VANET.
questions.detailed_solution: Ad-hoc is a domain of VANET.

questions.question: What are the disadvantages of V2X communication?
questions.detailed_solution:
Disadvantages of V2X communication includes tracking of movement,
violation of privacy, loss of data control, etc.

questions.question: What are some of the other names of the smart grids?
questions.detailed_solution:
Smart grids are also known as Electricity with a brain, Energy internet, and
Electronet.

questions.question: Smart Grid follows which type of the flow of energy?
questions.detailed_solution:
Smart grid is a modernized grid that enables bidirectional flow of
energy.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a benefit associated with smart grids?
questions.detailed_solution:
Smart Grids provide efficient transmission of electricity, lower
electricity rates and Improved security.

questions.question: Which of the following is a component of smart grid communication?
questions.detailed_solution:
The components of Smart Grid are Smart meters, Gateways,
DAUs and MDMSs

questions.question: Fill in the blank. ___________ can be used as an energy source in on-peak hours.
questions.detailed_solution:
PEVs or Plug-In Electric Vehicles can be used as an energy source in
on-peak hours

questions.question: Which of the following is a type of Dynamic System Attacks?
questions.detailed_solution:
Replay Attacks, Dynamic data injection attacks and Covert attacks are
all types of Dynamic System Attacks

questions.question: Which of the following statements are true about gateways? Statement I: Each gateway connects a few closely located smart meters. Statement II: Gateways communicate mostly based on WiFi. Statement III: They help in two-way communication.
questions.detailed_solution:
The gateways connect a few closely located smart meters, communicate
mostly on WiFi and help in two-way communication.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. ____________ is a centralized coordinator for smart grid communication.
questions.detailed_solution: MDMS is a centralized coordinator for smart grid communication.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. ____________ are available protocols for smart home appliances.
questions.detailed_solution: C-Bus, DECT, EnOcean and Universal Powerline Bus

questions.question: Fill in the blank. IIoT is a network of____________.
questions.detailed_solution:
IIoT is a network of physical objects, systems, platforms and
applications

questions.question: IIoT is based on Rip & Replace approach.
questions.detailed_solution: IIoT is based on wrap & re-use approach.

questions.question: Which of the following happened in the 1st Revolution?
questions.detailed_solution:
Mass production, internet evolution, automation happened in forth
revolution.

questions.question: Which of the following is a challenge of IIoT?
questions.detailed_solution:
Worker health and safety, environmental production and optimized
operations are all the safety challenges of IIoT.

questions.question: Monitoring and restoration of the traditional electrical grid is done manually.
questions.detailed_solution:
Monitoring and restoration of the traditional electrical grid is done
manually

questions.question: What is Hadoop used in?
questions.detailed_solution:
Hadoop is used for distributed processing of large datasets across
Large clusters of computers

questions.question: The two types of data analysis are _____________.
questions.detailed_solution:
Qualitative and Quantitative are the two types of data analysis. (Please
refer

questions.question: Which of the following is a principle of Qualitative analysis?
questions.detailed_solution:
The three principles of Qualitative analysis are: Notice things, think
about things and collect things.

questions.question: Select the statement(s) that denote the type of ANOVA. Statement I: One way analysis Statement II: Two way analysis Statement III: K-way analysis
questions.detailed_solution:
The types of ANOVA includes One way analysis, Two way analysis
and K-way analysis

questions.question: What is the type of the data dispersion?
questions.detailed_solution:
Range, average absolute deviation and variation are the type of data
dispersions.

questions.question: The process by which numerical data is analyzed is known as __________.
questions.detailed_solution:
Quantitative analysis is the process by which numerical data is
analyzed.

questions.question: The mathematical equation that is formulated in the form of relationships between variables is known as __________.
questions.detailed_solution:
The mathematical equation that is formulated in the form of
relationships between variables is known as Statistical model

questions.question: What are the two types of statistical models?
questions.detailed_solution:
Complete and incomplete are two types of statistical models (Please
refer

questions.question: Contingency table is also known as?
questions.detailed_solution: Contingency table is also known as Cross tabulation and Cross table.

questions.question: Advances in sensor and connectivity have disabled preventive care.
questions.detailed_solution: Advances in sensor and connectivity have disabled preventive care.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of IoT Healthcare?.
questions.detailed_solution:
Non-invasive monitoring, Cloud-based analytics and Wireless
transmission are all types of IoT healthcare.

questions.question: Which of the following are components of IoT?
questions.detailed_solution:
Sensing layer, Aggregated layer, Processing layer and cloud platform
are components of IoT.

questions.question: Which of the following is the advantage of activity monitoring?
questions.detailed_solution: Long term monitoring is one of the advantages of Activity monitoring.

questions.question: Deep learning based data analysis cannot be performed on videos
questions.detailed_solution:
Deep learning based data analysis can be done on sensor data,
images and videos.

questions.question: Which of the following is true for in-place activity monitoring data analysis?
questions.detailed_solution: In-place activity monitoring data analysis is on-device and power intensive

questions.question: Fill in the blank. Processing the handheld activity device data with artificial intelligence can be used for ________.
questions.detailed_solution:
Processing the handheld activity device data with artificial intelligence
can be used for detecting sudden fall of a person.

source_name: NPTEL - Introduction to Internet of Things

exam_pattern.practice_scoring_model: 1 mark per question inside this practice app

certification_weightage_hint.course_note: NPTEL course pages show that best-assignment aggregation varies by course, while certification commonly uses 25% assignment and 75% proctored exam weightage.
